cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer. The question is about the 1974 World Bank study mentioned in the passage. The key point is what the study concluded regarding the benefits of economic growth in developing countries. First, I'll go back to the relevant part of the text. The passage says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So the study found that even though these countries had rapid growth, the benefit was only to a third of the population. That means the majority (two-thirds) didn't get much benefit.
